channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Archdiocese of Los Angeles agrees to pay $880 million to victims of clergy sexual abuse
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The Archdiocese of Los Angeles has agreed to pay $880 million to victims of clergy sexual abuse dating back decades, in what an attorney said was the largest single child sex abuse settlement with a Catholic archdiocese, it was announced Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: A Texas board rejects clemency plea from a man facing execution in shaken baby syndrome case
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — A Texas state board on Wednesday declined to stop what could be the first execution in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: JUAN A. LOZANO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Navy jet located after crashing near Mount Rainier in Washington
item.description: The U.S. Navy located an EA-18G Growler jet on Wednesday that crashed near Mount Rainier in Washington on Tuesday, though the status of the two-person crew is unknown.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden delivers final eulogy during Ethel Kennedy memorial service in DC
item.description: Ethel Kennedy, 96, died last week. She had been hospitalized after suffering a stroke earlier this month.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, WGN 9

item.title: Head of nonprofit that promotes school 'segregation' received $20M from Gates, Bezos foundations, among others
item.description: The founder of a nonprofit that advocates for more Black teachers received $20 million in funding from Bill Gates and other wealthy donors and corporations.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 3 dead, at least 4 injured after bridge collapses in Mississippi
item.description: Three people have died and at least four were injured after a bridge collapsed in Mississippi on Wednesday, according to authorities.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Smith, KREX 5

item.title: Family of Menendez brothers demands their release from prison
item.description: The family of Lyle and Erik Menendez, as well as their attorney, held a press conference Wednesday to discuss prosecutors’ review of their convictions for murdering their parents, José and Kitty Menendez, in Beverly Hills in 1989.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, ABC 4

item.title: Navy finds wreckage of fighter jet that crashed during training in Washington state
item.description: The U.S. Navy located the wreckage Wednesday of a fighter jet that crashed in Washington state during a routine training flight in mountainous terrain. The two crew members onboard remain missing.
item.dc:creator: MARTHA BELLISLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge invalidates election rules passed by Trump-backed board in Georgia
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — A Georgia judge has declared that seven new election rules recently passed by the State Election Board are “illegal, unconstitutional and void.” Fulton County Superior Court Judge Thomas Cox issued the order Wednesday after holding a hearing on challenges to the rules.
item.dc:creator: KATE BRUMBACK, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Biden eulogizes Ethel Kennedy as 'hero' who put her own stamp on country
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden eulogized the late Ethel Kennedy in deeply personal terms at a memorial service Wednesday, hailing the wife of the late Sen. Robert F.
item.dc:creator: KEVIN FREKING and DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: La Nina could soon arrive. Here's what that means for winter weather
item.description: The National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration’s Climate Prediction Center says there is a 60% chance that a weak La Nina event will develop this autumn and could last until March.
item.dc:creator: ISABELLA O'MALLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pro-Israel professor at Columbia barred from campus after harassing and intimidating other employees
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — An outspoken pro-Israel professor at Columbia University has been temporarily barred from campus, a university spokesperson said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: PHILIP MARCELO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Federal money to help states hit by hurricanes Helene and Milton nears $2 billion
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The federal government has approved nearly $2 billion in assistance across six states to help people hit hard by hurricanes Helene and Milton, the White House said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Killer's escape from Pennsylvania jail costs him his right to appeal life sentence
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — A convicted killer’s escape from a Pennsylvania jail last year has cost him his right to appeal his original life sentence, a state court ruled Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: California health care workers get a pay bump under a new minimum wage law
item.description: SACRAMENTO, Calif. (AP) — Some of the lowest-paid health care workers in California will get a pay bump Wednesday under a state law gradually increasing their wages to at least $25 an hour.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIE AUSTIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: ISIS-K orchestrated foiled Election Day terror plot: US official
item.description: Afghan native Nasir Ahmad Tawhedi, 27, was arrested in early October and accused of planning to target people on Election Day.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, KRON4

item.title: Listeria recall grows to 12 million pounds of meat and poultry, some of it sent to US schools
item.description: A nationwide recall of meat and poultry products potentially contaminated with listeria has expanded to nearly 12 million pounds and now includes ready-to-eat meals sent to U.S. schools, restaurants and major retailers, federal officials said.
item.dc:creator: JONEL ALECCIA, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Biden delivers final eulogy during Ethel Kennedy memorial service in DC
item.description: Ethel Kennedy, 96, died last week. She had been hospitalized after suffering a stroke earlier this month.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Clint Eastwood's daughter arrested in Beverly Hills in domestic violence case
item.description: Actor Francesca Eastwood, daughter of legendary actor Clint Eastwood, was arrested in Beverly Hills over the weekend on suspicion of felony domestic violence, according to reporting from the Los Angeles Times.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, ABC 4

item.title: Navy: Fighter jet with two on board crashed in Washington state during training
item.description: The U.S. Navy was searching for two aviators who went missing after their plane crashed during a routine training flight in Washington state.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Secret Service trained at mock White House that Hollywood producer Tyler Perry built as stage
item.description: The U.S. Secret Service used Tyler Perry's mock White House in Atlanta for a training scenario in 2023 as it works to construct its own White House training facility.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: FEMA workers threatened by armed group in Tennessee
item.description: "They were all armed, open carry not guns drawn, but they had surrounded them and there was a lady there that was yelling and threatening them.
item.dc:creator: Karen Jenkins, ABC 4

item.title: Amazon, Google make dueling nuclear investments to power data centers with clean energy
item.description: Amazon on Wednesday said that it was investing in small nuclear reactors, coming just two days after a similar announcement by Google, as both tech giants seek new sources of carbon-free electricity to meet surging demand from data centers and artificial intelligence.
item.dc:creator: ALEXA ST. JOHN and JENNIFER McDERMOTT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Michelle Obama will headline an Atlanta rally aimed at boosting voter turnout
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Former first lady Michelle Obama will headline a rally in Atlanta a week before the Nov. 5 election alongside celebrities and civic leaders focusing on engaging younger and first-time voters, as well as voters of color. The Oct.
item.dc:creator: MATT BROWN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Republicans challenge more than 63,000 voters in Georgia, but few removed, AP finds
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — From Georgia's mountains to its Atlantic shore, challenges to the qualifications of voters have rolled in this summer and fall, part of a wide-ranging national effort coordinated by Donald Trump’s allies to enlist Republican activists to remove people they view as suspect from the vot
item.dc:creator: JEFF AMY and CHARLOTTE KRAMON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Man to row across the Pacific Ocean to honor victims of war in Ukraine
item.description: Aurimas Mockus from Lithuania has embarked on a historic journey across the Pacific Ocean from San Diego to Australia in a solo rowing boat, aiming to be the first person in the world to complete the 12,000 kilometer trip alone, without help or stops.
item.dc:creator: Dan Plante, Fox 5

item.title: Hurricane misinformation stokes fears ahead of election
item.description: The flood of conspiracy theories and rumors following the severe weather in the South is sparking concerns that political figures’ embrace of misinformation is just the beginning as the general election draws closer.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Rufus Wainwright blasts Trump playing 'Hallelujah' at town hall: 'The height of blasphemy'
item.description: The 51-year-old singer said Tuesday that he was "horrified" to learn his 2001 cover of Leonard Cohen's "Hallelujah" was part of the playlist at the Trump campaign event a night earlier.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, KRON4

item.title: Country singer pauses concert as wife gives birth on tour bus
item.description: Country singer Brantley Gilbert's wife, Amber, welcomed their third child on a tour bus in the middle of his concert on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, ABC 4

item.title: Bath & Body Works apologizes for candle designed with what looked like KKK hoods
item.description: Bath & Body Works apologized for selling a winter-themed candle that came under scrutiny for label imagery that drew comparisons to Ku Klux Klan hoods, according to multiple reports.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, ABC 4

item.title: Group tied to polygamous sect builds fences on US Forest Service land
item.description: A group in Colorado has fenced a parcel of U.S. Forest Service Land in an attempt to claim the land under the Homestead Act of 1862. According to the Montezuma County Sheriff's Office, the actions have resulted in a property dispute between the Free Land Holders and the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Heather Willard, KTLA 5

item.title: Families seek closure in Texas Killing Fields murders in an all-new episode of ‘The Evidence Room’
item.description: A new episode of the KPRC 2 Investigates true crime docuseries 'The Evidence Room' premieres Wednesday at 6:30 p.m. exclusively on the KPRC 2+ livestream.
item.dc:creator: Robert Arnold, Tiffani Lupenski, Ninfa Saavedra, KPRC 2

item.title: Armed man arrested near Trump rally denies 'assassination attempt' claims
item.description: “I’m an artist, I’m the last person that would cause any violence and harm to anybody,” the man has since said.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, Fox 5

item.title: Group associated with polygamous sect builds fences on US Forest Service lands
item.description: A group in Colorado has fenced a parcel of U.S. Forest Service Land in an attempt to claim the land under the Homestead Act of 1862.
item.dc:creator: Heather Willard, Fox 5

item.title: Man with loaded gun arrested at checkpoint near Donald Trump's weekend rally in Southern California
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — A Nevada man with a shotgun, loaded handgun, ammunition and several fake passports in his vehicle was arrested at a security checkpoint outside Donald Trump's rally Saturday night in the Southern California desert, authorities said. He was released the same day on $5,000 bail.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER WEBER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: What $35 trillion in US national debt means for Americans
item.description: With the national debt standing at more than $35 trillion, economists fear that the country's debt load will only grow.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, ABC 4

item.title: Prison operator under federal scrutiny spent millions settling Tennessee mistreatment claims
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — The leading private prison company in the U.S. has spent more than $4.4 million to settle dozens of complaints alleging mistreatment — including at least 22 inmate deaths — at its Tennessee prisons and jails since 2016.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN MATTISE, TRAVIS LOLLER and KRISTIN M. HALL, Associated Press, ABC 4
